article_meta.title: Trump strategy document revives Monroe Doctrine, slams EuropeBy Idrees Ali, Matt Spetalnick and James MackenzieDecember

reasoning: The model does a good job overall. It correctly identifies genuine bias patterns in the author's own prose and avoids flagging quoted text. The identified instances are defensible, with appropriate bias type labels. There are minor debatable points but no systematic failures.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All five flagged items come from the author's own prose - headline, connecting narrative, and framing sentences. No quoted text or attributed source statements are flagged as the author's bias. The model correctly handles the numerous document quotes and source quotes throughout the article.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most items are defensible. 'slams Europe' is genuinely loaded headline language. 'troubled history' is a subjective characterization. 'increasingly racist rhetoric' is a strong editorial judgment in the author's voice. The two Slant items are somewhat debatable - the critics/imperialism passage does include some balance ('He spoke early on, in vague terms') and the Russia passage does include the 'soft on Russia' framing in quotes. These are reasonable but not ironclad flags.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model catches most significant instances. It could potentially have also flagged 'dour view' as a subjective qualifying adjective (author characterizes the administration's stance), and 'The document is the latest - and clearest - expression' as editorializing. The 'sweeping changes could revive suspicion and consternation' in the summary bullets is also arguably author-level framing. Minor misses.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Most labels are appropriate. 'slams' as Word Choice is correct. 'troubled history' as Word Choice is defensible. The 'racist rhetoric' item could arguably be 'Opinion Statements Presented as Fact' rather than just Word Choice, since the author presents a contested characterization as fact. The Slant labels for the two structural emphasis items are reasonable. Minor quibbles but nothing clearly wrong.
